title: pedicularis bracteosa subsp. paysoniana
intro: Pedicularis bracteosa subsp. paysoniana, also known as Payson's lousewort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bumblebee balm family (Orobanchaceae). This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Payson's lousewort has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and bronchodilator properties, providing relief from coughs and congestion. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
